input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when the dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates on theme swap -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by replacing/merging dictionaries at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Activity restart on system dark mode change.**
Android destroys and recreates the Activity on `uiMode` changes. In your `MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme", MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                  | ConfigChanges.UiMode | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                  | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This prevents the restart. You can then detect the system change and swap your theme programmatically:

```csharp
protected override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);
    var isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource`, and add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to your Activity attribute.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors from their ResourceDictionary themes. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the Android system dark mode changes, the Activity is destroyed and recreated by default. In .NET MAUI, they need to handle the `ConfigChanges` flag to prevent this.

Let me provide a concise answer covering both issues.
